description: Make @type a noted scoping concern. In define_scope FIRST walk (where fns/modules/protocols are reserved so forward refs resolve), reserve each @type from the surface attrs: build TypeName{module,name,arity}, bind NamespaceSymbol::Type(TypeName) into the scope, and note its declaration -- the parsed in-house TypeDefBody (.11) plus the namespace captured at that point -- into a TypeName->decl map on World. Reserving in the first walk puts sibling/forward type names in scope, so the old build_module_type_env fixpoint is unnecessary. Add NamespaceSymbol::Type(TypeName). Retain top-level @type the way modules keep theirs: finish_code_index (source.rs:58) drops program.attrs while modules keep theirs (source.rs:492) -- make GLOBAL uniform so a top-level @type reserves into the GLOBAL scope. No reference walk, no resolution here. Verify: namespace_test extended for Type; a @type becomes a referenceable identity with its TypeDefBody + captured namespace held; a top-level @type lands in GLOBAL.

comments.text: When wiring this in-house parser, REMOVE the module-level #![allow(dead_code)] at the top of src/compiler2/type_expr.rs -- it marks the not-yet-consumed parser from .11 and must come off once the reference walk (.1) and resolver (.3) consume it.

title: Note @type declarations: TypeName, NamespaceSymbol::Type, hold body+namespace, retain top-level @type
